title: Question about initial viewing of Evangelion
display_title: Question about initial viewing of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Question about initial viewing of Evangelion.             Quote Post by lostpainting » Sun Jul 20, 2008 4:17 pm Hi everyone, I'm new to Evangelion. I've never seen the series or EoE before. I wanted to ask people's opinions about how many episodes I should watch at a time for my initial viewing. Sure, I'll end up watching however many episodes I want at the moment depending on how I feel, but it doesn't hurt to ask for opinions. I'll be watching it over the course of a few nights(hopefully no more than a week). Would watching the whole series plus EoE in roughly even segments over the course of 3 nights be taking on too much at a time? I know that I wouldn't even begin to scratch the surface of the iceberg as far as understanding it goes during my initial viewings, which is fine, so it's not like I'll be trying to decipher much. But as far as having a visceral experience, is this still too much? Just wanted to see what fans might suggest. Sorry if it's considered bad to start two topics concurrently, but I felt that asking about whether or not EoE was still out of print, or available anywhere for that matter(my other topic) was a separate issue.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Jul 20, 2008 4:30 pm That sounds like overkill, trying to watch everything in just three days. If I were you, I'd space things out a bit. Maybe watch four episodes a day, and then EoE on a separate day after you finish up the series. You don't want to marathon the thing, zone out in parts, and miss stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by zimlord » Sun Jul 20, 2008 7:27 pm I agree with Brik. If you watch four or five episodes a day, and then EoE on its own night, you'll have it finished in a week, which is better than chewing more than you can swallow or waiting too long in between eps. You're gonna want to take it slow and find plenty of time to fanwank.

[]:             Quote Post by lostpainting » Sun Jul 20, 2008 9:11 pm Thanks for the responses, you two. I think you're right, 4 or 5 episodes per night does sound like the better idea after all. And I'll give EoE it's own night, too.

[]:             Quote Post by Super Chris » Mon Jul 21, 2008 12:38 am Pacing yourself is fine, but on the rare occasion that I have the time, I like to watch it all in one sitting. (This is true of any series, not just Evangelion.) If I might make a suggestion for the ending, is after episode 24, start End of Evangelion. During "My Purest Heart for You", stop the movie right as soon as the live action sequence begins, and watch episode 25 of the series. After that, finish the live action montage, then watch 26 of the series. After that, watch the end of the movie. I've done this twice with friends who hadn't seen the series before, and both times their reactions at the end were "Oh!" rather than the reaction I usually got before, "What?"

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jul 21, 2008 1:09 am You know you're doing it wrong if the response is not "What the fuck did I just watch?"

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jul 21, 2008 2:17 am Super Chris: While your suggestion is good, what I'd personally recommend is watching all of Episode 25 of the series ending, then EoE 25' Air. Then Episode 26 series and finally EoE 26' My Pure Heart for you. This isn't just because the ending(s) can make more sense this way, but also because that's how they were orignally released in Japan: http://www.pustan.com/ld/Japan/nge.html http://www.pustan.com/ld/Japan/nge013.html http://www.pustan.com/ld/Japan/nge014.html TV25 with Movie25, then TV26 with Movie26. (Though it should be noted the version of EoE on this release was the Video version, eg, this: http://www.angelfire.com/anime4/mdwigs/Movies.html ) Just a thought, at least.

[]:             Quote Post by Super Chris » Tue Jul 22, 2008 12:39 am Since we're talking about a first viewing I'll warn that this post could be considered to contain spoilers, and I'm not sure if there's a spoilers tag so they won't be hidden. Then again, there is after all a statute of limitations on this kind of thing, too. (P.S. - King Kong dies.) Anyway, that's a pretty good idea too, SSD. The main thing, I would say, is to watch it in such a way that it becomes one cohesive ending, rather than seeming like two separate endings. The way I suggested is more of a stream of reasoning thing for Shinji - in EoE, he kind of just jumps from "I want everyone to die!" to "Well, I guess other people aren't so bad after all" with very little reasoning in between. The way I suggested kind of follows the arc of, "I thought this would be a world without pain" (Third Impact happens) to "This world, with only you in it, is the world you wished for" (Ep. 25) to "Well, why can't I dream I'm alone then?" (Montage) to "But I'm a bad person... well, maybe not" (Ep. 26) to "That being the case, being with others is worthwhile too" (Finale of EoE). Actually, if I was willing to do (a lot) more disc switching, then I'd probably say ep. 24, to the first half of EoTV Ep. 25 (Shinji's guilt, as well as the first part of Rei's and Asuka's instrumentality, which to me seem to happen in preinstrumentality, during 'Air'), then Ep. 25' (the battle of Armageddon as I refer to it), then Ep. 26' up until the end of Komm Susser Tod (Third Impact), second half of Ep. 25 (Instumentality is occurring, empty world), EoE Montage (Why can't I dream I'm alone argument), Ep. 26 (The resolution of Shinji's primary crisis), then the rest of EoE (return to the real world). I'd even consider throwing the deleted scene that originally went in the spot now taken up by the Live Action montage in at the halfway point of 26. (EoTV) Ridiculously overcomplicated, yes, and more disc switching than I'm willing to do in that short a time. But, at least to me, it would flow best watching in that order.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jul 22, 2008 1:09 am Super Chris wrote: Since we're talking about a first viewing I'll warn that this post could be considered to contain spoilers, and I'm not sure if there's a spoilers tag so they won't be hidden.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jul 22, 2008 8:16 am Super Chris wrote: Fanwankery

[]:             Quote Post by lostpainting » Tue Jul 22, 2008 8:51 am Thanks for all of the replies. I almost forgot to ask: Does the fan opinion seem to be unanimous in that I should watch the directors cuts the first time around rather than the original versions of those episodes?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jul 22, 2008 12:54 pm Eva Monkey wrote: You're trying to ruin Evangelion for him, aren't you?

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Tue Jul 22, 2008 1:00 pm Normally, people will watch it, you know... from beginning straight to the end; anything else is left to you to try to "understand better" the whole narrative. ... Not like anyone does, but people seem to like to think they can grasp the "essence of Evangelion" and toot their fingers, since they obviously posses "the one real truth!" The Evamonkey wrote: that's just a bunch of argumentative fan bullshit.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Jul 22, 2008 2:39 pm when I first saw the series, I watched through episode 24....then watched End of Eva, skipping episodes 25 and 26 on the advice of a friend. And I didn't bother to see the last two episodes for months after that. They're simply not the originally intended or "actual" ending.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Tue Jul 22, 2008 3:00 pm lostpainting wrote: Thanks for all of the replies. I almost forgot to ask: Does the fan opinion seem to be unanimous in that I should watch the directors cuts the first time around rather than the original versions of those episodes?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jul 22, 2008 3:35 pm cat42 wrote: I say skip the original cuts entirely. They are unnecessary in my opinion.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jul 22, 2008 3:47 pm The original version of the OA version can't be even watched unless you get something other than Renewal/Platinum though: [url]http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?p=156247#156247[/url] wrote: In the transition from OA to DC, a lot of cuts were redone in some way (the cel portions, completely from scratch, etc.). In the 21 OA and 22 OA on Renewal, the old animation present from the original versions of these episodes is almost entirely absent, having been replaced with the DC retakes. This eliminates much of the point of having the OAs on there at all, don't it?

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Tue Jul 22, 2008 3:47 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Am I the only one that appreciates the original, and understands that the directors cuts can't be appreciated without a basis of reference?

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Tue Jul 22, 2008 3:48 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Am I the only one that appreciates the original, and understands that the directors cuts can't be appreciated without a basis of reference?

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Tue Jul 22, 2008 3:52 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: [url]http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?p=156247#156247[/url] wrote: In the transition from OA to DC, a lot of cuts were redone in some way (the cel portions, completely from scratch, etc.). In the 21 OA and 22 OA on Renewal, the old animation present from the original versions of these episodes is almost entirely absent, having been replaced with the DC retakes. This eliminates much of the point of having the OAs on there at all, don't it?
